title: Ensure that containers in pods properly set hostname
body:
When we moved to the new Namespace types in Specgen, we made a distinction between taking a namespace from a pod, and taking it from another container. Due to this new distinction, some code
that previously worked for both `--pod=$ID` and `--uts=container:$ID` has accidentally become conditional on only the latter case. This happened for Hostname - we weren't properly setting it in cases where the container joined a pod. Fortunately, this is an easy fix once we know to check the condition.

Also, ensure that `podman pod inspect` actually prints hostname.

Fixes #6494

Second commit pushed with further fixes. This resolves the issue, as tested on a RHEL8 CI VM provided by @edsantiago 

The issue is the presence of a `containers.conf` file. We need to make sure we have a default containers.conf on our CI VMs so we catch things like this.
@rhatdan PTAL. I don't think you'll like the second commit, but I don't see a better way without a big rewrite of the specgen population code.

I am fine with this change. LGTM
I think this is the best for now although we could add some hanlding of remote to github.com/containers, so it only returns the values when grabbing locally.
@vrothberg @baude @jwhonce PTAL, let's get this merged, then.
